body.u: At the age of fifteen I was worrying about my exams. But when they did finally come they went all right in between time while they exams were coming up I would visit the carreerscareers office to find out if there was any more application for apprentice engineers or motor mechanic. The next thing was my interviews all of these went well and I think I pleased every firm. Then my sixteen birthday came which was {fun?} a couple of mates came round and we had a drink and played snooker a couple of days later I recievedreceived my results of my cse exams. They were maths 3, English 3, physical science 3, local studies, 2, Art 2, metal work 2, tech drawing 3, so overall I was pleased then on the 30th of August I received a letter. I opened it and read through it. I began to smile the letter said I had suceededsucceeded in the interview at Bowaters and had got the job as apprentice vehicle mechanic at the lorry yard and could I start my course on the 2nd September 1978.

On the 2nd I went to Sittingbourne at the Bowater lorry depot and me my boss and workmates then after we had dinner we had a talk about weather I had any transport as the first year will be off the job training. I told him I had more. So I took the train journey to Maidstone so he could show me where to go when we got there he introduced me to some lecturers and told me I would either travel each day or get lodging and I suggested I might be able to stay at my sister and brother-in laws place. I got things sorted out and the first year went realyreally quick and in the test at college I done realyreally well. Then I started training at the yard itself. When my seventeenth birthday came my mum and dad had brought me a second hand mini so this was my year to be but I failed my first test but got through on the second go. The next three years went quick and only 1 bad thing happened and that was when I was working on an engine and I fell and twisted my ankle really badly which put me out for a couple of months.

Then the day came when I had served my full four years as an apprentice and I received my sitting guilds and the certificate to say I had served my apprenticeship. Then I went onto the workshop floor for the next day as a mechanic along with the other mechanics that were already there. When I was twenty two I got married to Jenny and we moved to Sittingbourne. The at 35 I was made assistant mechanic then I served Ten years as that then at forty five I was made chief mechanic then I stayed as chief mechanic until 65.
